title: Refactor /api/auto-tag to parallel detector evaluation + single tag writer
description: Parallelize the read/compute side of auto-tag (the detector loop in analysis/auto_tag.py) while keeping DjmdMyTag / DjmdSongMyTag writes serialized on a single writer thread.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auto-tag (multi-type) and /api/auto-tag/discogs (SSE) still produce identical tag rows compared to the v0 path on the same library.
Throughput >= 100 tracks/sec on a 10k-track library when classification is cache-warm.
Per-track exceptions emit an error event; the run continues; undo_data remains correct.
Existing tests in tests/test_auto_tag.py and tests/test_serve_routes.py auto-tag cases stay green.

steps.details: In analysis/auto_tag.py find apply_tags(). Pull the per-track detector evaluation into _eval_one(content, db, requested_types) returning {content_id, tag_names_to_apply, existing_tag_ids}. _eval_one does no writes.
Submit _eval_one through the pool. Push results into a queue.Queue read by a writer thread.

steps.description: Single writer thread for DjmdMyTag / DjmdSongMyTag
steps.details: Writer drains the queue: for each result, ensure_tag_by_name() for any new tag, then insert DjmdSongMyTag rows for the (content, tag) pairs, commit per track, push to undo_data. SSE event emitted after commit.

/api/auto-tag/discogs uses discogs.search_styles which is rate-limited (60 req/min, token-bucket). The pool will burst-call - that's fine, the token bucket throttles. But ensure the pool size is independent of the Discogs rate: the bucket caps effective throughput. Wire identical pattern.

Update tests/test_auto_tag.py: assertions on undo_data should still hold even with parallel evaluation (undo entries written in writer order, not eval order). Add a test running auto-tag then auto-tag/undo and verify zero rows added/remain.
RUN_PERF=1 pytest tests/perf/test_auto_tag.py - confirm >=100 tracks/sec warm. Also confirm undo throughput >=200 tracks/sec (writes only).

technicalNotes: ensure_category_tags / ensure_tag_by_name create-or-reuse tags by name idempotently - safe to call from the writer thread; the writer is the only thread touching DjmdMyTag rows.
apply_classification_tags() (single-category path) follows the same pattern; refactor in the same task if straightforward.
